title: cogswellia howellii
intro: Cogswellia howellii, also known as the Howell's Desert Currant, is a flowering plant that belongs to the family Capparaceae. It is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, and its small yellow flowers are a valuable source of medicinal properties. The plant contains flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. Cogswellia howellii can be used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are said to promote digestive health, boost the immune system, and reduce inflammation.
